title: Table data is lost on DB restart, but only when a table has 2147483647 or more rows.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(test_column INT NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW());

INSERT INTO test_table(test_column, name) SELECT x, 'Test Name' FROM (SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS x FROM (SELECT 1 AS dummy UNION ALL SELECT 1) AS a, (SELECT 1 AS dummy UNION ALL SELECT 1) AS b) AS numbers WHERE x <= 2147483647;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE test_column IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(test_column) FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Data loss on DB restart with large table row count
